title: Timing, Resource Graphs, Transfer, Caching, and User-Performance Boundaries · Program Brief: Resource Inventory Graph
summary: The audit calls server response time page speed, double-counts cached resources, ignores compression and render work, and reports one lab run as user experience. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the resource inventory graph increment in bounded page-resource and timing audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Classify document, stylesheet, script, image, font, media, API, iframe, preload, and redirect resources with initiator and origin.
Separate queue, DNS, connect, TLS, request, first-byte, body, parse, render, and total timing with clock and instrumentation limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-resource-inventory-graph — Classify document, stylesheet, script, image, font, media, API, iframe, preload, and redirect resources with initiator and origin.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-performance-resource-audit-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-performance-resource-audit-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_performance_resource_audit_4(pages: int = 12, resources: int = 180, transfer_bytes: int = 2400000, expanded_bytes: int = 6700000, user_experience_claims: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or resources < pages:
        return False, resources, "resource-count"
    if transfer_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < transfer_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "transfer-accounting"
    if user_experience_claims > 0:
        return False, user_experience_claims, "user-performance-overclaim"
    if resources > 1000:
        return False, resources, "resource-budget"
    assert expanded_bytes >= transfer_bytes and user_experience_claims == 0
    return True, resources, "bounded-resource-audit"
